content:
💡 CatVTON | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Virtual clothing try-on for fashion
✨ Key Features: • Lightweight Network (899.06M parameters) • Parameter-Efficient Training (49.57M parameters trainable) • Simplified Inference (< 8G VRAM for 1024x768) • Diffusion Model for Virtual Try-On • Mask-free version available
📖 Summary: CatVTON is an efficient diffusion model for virtual clothing try-on. It features a lightweight network architecture, parameter-efficient training, and simplified inference, allowing high-resolution try-ons with minimal VRAM.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 tickrs | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Displaying real-time stock market data and charts in a terminal environment.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time ticker data display • Terminal User Interface (TUI) • Data sourced from Yahoo! Finance • Customizable chart types (line, candle, kagi) • Configurable time frames and update intervals
📖 Summary: Tickrs is a Rust-based command-line application that provides real-time stock ticker data directly in the terminal. It leverages Yahoo! Finance for data and offers various customization options for displaying stock information, charts, and market hours.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ seeker | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: To demonstrate and educate users about how malicious websites can exploit location permissions and gather sensitive device information through social engineering, acting as a proof-of-concept for security awareness.
✨ Key Features: • Hosts fake websites to request location permissions
📖 Summary: Seeker is a tool designed to accurately locate smartphones by hosting a fake website that requests location permissions. Upon user consent, it gathers precise location data (longitude, latitude, accuracy, altitude) and device information (unique ID, model, OS, hardware specs, IP addresses) through social engineering tactics. It serves as a proof-of-concept to educate users about potential data privacy risks from malicious websites.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 NeoShell | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Remotely managing and controlling a Windows PC from a smartphone or other web-enabled device over a local Wi-Fi network.
✨ Key Features: • Remote PC control via Wi-Fi • Web-based interface • PC locking, sleep, shutdown, reboot • Minimize all windows • Task Manager access
📖 Summary: NeoShell is a Python-based application that allows users to remotely control their Windows PC from a smartphone via Wi-Fi. It provides a web-based interface accessible from any device on the same network, offering features like PC locking, sleep, shutdown, task management, and application launching.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Gource | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Visualizing and understanding the history and evolution of source code repositories in an engaging and interactive 3D environment.
✨ Key Features: • 3D visualization of repository history • Tree-based display of directories and files • Contributor avatars appearing/disappearing • Interactive playback controls (start/stop dates, speed) • Customizable appearance (colors, background, logo)
📖 Summary: Gource is a C++ visualization tool that renders source control repository history as an interactive 3D tree. It visually represents directories as branches and files as leaves, with contributors appearing and disappearing as they make changes. The tool allows for real-time playback and customization of the visualization.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
